education.other_schools_training_name: Oregon Department of Public Safety Standards and Training (DPSST) Basic Police Academy
education.title_description_of_course: DPSST Basic Police Academy – comprehensive law enforcement basic training including criminal and traffic law, defensive tactics, firearms, emergency vehicle operations, report writing, cultural competency, crisis intervention, ethics, and community policing. The program included scenario-based training (e.g., domestic disturbance calls, traffic stops, field interviews), legal updates from district attorneys, and extensive practical exercises in de-escalation, use of force decision-making, and evidence handling. I completed all required written exams, physical fitness benchmarks, and practical evaluations, including a final multi-scenario assessment day. I also participated in supplemental evening study groups focusing on Oregon Revised Statutes Title 16 (Crimes and Punishments) and Title 59 (Oregon Vehicle Code) to strengthen my working knowledge of relevant state law.

professional_licenses_registration_or_ce.licensing_agency_name_and_complete_addre: Oregon Department of Public Safety Standards and Training (DPSST), 4190 Aumsville Hwy SE, Salem, OR 97317
professional_licenses_registration_or_ce.endorsement_restriction_if_applicable: No restrictions; active status. Required to complete ongoing annual in-service training (minimum 40 hours per year) including firearms qualification, legal updates, and use-of-force refresher training.

special_skills.computer_software: Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook; Spillman CAD/RMS; Axon Evidence.com; Tyler Technologies public safety suite; Adobe Acrobat; basic use of Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive); LEDS/NCIC terminal interfaces; basic usage of digital dictation and transcription software for report drafting.
special_skills.computer_languages: Basic familiarity with SQL for running simple queries on records systems; introductory-level Python (used for personal data analysis projects, not in an official capacity).
special_skills.other_special_skills_line_1: Fluent in English; conversational Spanish (able to conduct basic field interviews, provide simple instructions during traffic stops, and explain rights and forms at a basic level; routinely request certified interpreters for complex or sensitive matters).
special_skills.other_special_skills_line_2: Extensive experience with crisis intervention and de-escalation techniques, particularly with individuals experiencing mental health crises, substance intoxication, or homelessness. Completed multiple 40-hour Crisis Intervention Team (CIT) trainings and have served informally as a resource officer for newer officers when handling high-stress calls involving suicidal subjects or emotionally disturbed persons.
special_skills.other_special_skills_line_3: Strong report-writing and documentation skills, with emphasis on clear chronological narratives, precise articulation of probable cause, and thorough articulation of use-of-force decision-making. Regularly requested by supervisors to assist newer officers in tightening up their incident reports and search warrant affidavits, and known for turning around complex case reports (e.g., multi-vehicle collisions or multi-witness assaults) within required deadlines.
special_skills.other_special_skills_line_4: Comfortable providing testimony in municipal and county courts; have testified in DUII, domestic violence, and theft-related cases, and have experience explaining investigative steps and evidence collection methods in plain language for judges and juries. Also experienced in community outreach presentations on personal safety, fraud prevention, and how to interact with law enforcement during traffic stops or emergency situations.

criminal_convictions.criminal_convictions_line_1: N/A – I have no adult criminal convictions in any jurisdiction. I have never been convicted of a felony or misdemeanor, nor have I entered a guilty or no-contest plea in any criminal matter.

equipment.equipment_you_can_operate_line_1: Patrol vehicles (marked and unmarked) including Ford Police Interceptor Utility and Dodge Charger; proficient in emergency vehicle operation, pursuit driving (within policy), and safe backing and parking in confined urban environments.
equipment.equipment_you_can_operate_line_2: In-car and body-worn camera systems (Axon/TASER platforms), mobile data terminals (MDTs) for CAD/RMS access, dash-mounted radar and LIDAR speed measurement devices, and basic mobile radio and handheld radio communication equipment.
equipment.equipment_you_can_operate_line_3: Standard law enforcement equipment: semi-automatic sidearm, patrol rifle (AR-15 platform), shotgun, TASER conducted energy weapon, OC spray, expandable baton, handcuffs, leg restraints, and basic evidence collection tools (e.g., fingerprint kits, evidence packaging and labeling tools).
equipment.equipment_you_can_operate_line_4: Office and administrative equipment: desktop and laptop computers (Windows-based), networked printers and scanners, photocopiers, digital recording devices for interviews, and basic AV equipment (projector, portable PA system) for community presentations.
equipment.equipment_you_can_operate_line_5: Basic first-aid and emergency response equipment, including AEDs, tourniquets, trauma dressings, and nasal Narcan administration kits; familiar with the layout and use of equipment stored in patrol cars and in supervisor vehicles (go-bags, riot gear containers, and traffic control devices such as flares and portable cones).
